item.title: Illegal logging will sink government's timber processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: EOI – International Industry Partnership Specialist (CS16)
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Department of Higher Education, Research Science and Technology (DHERST) through the Improved for TVET Employment Project is requesting Expressions of Interest for the following The post EOI – International Industry Partnership Specialist (CS16) first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Morobe’s New Tourism Authority Board
item.description: [Inside PNG] Morobe Governor and Provincial Executive Council (PEC) Chairman, Rainbo Paita, has announced the appointment of a new Morobe Tourism Authority Board following a decision of the Provincial Executive Council during its Sam Sewe Meeting No. 04/2026 held on 23–24 April 2026. The decision formally revoke

item.title: Community Volunteers Join PNG Police Crime Prevention Drive at Nine Mile Port Moresby
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Forty-five community volunteers have joined a new policing initiative in PNG's Nine Mile and Bomana areas aimed at strengthening cooperation between residents and law enforcement agencies. The volunteers were introduced during the launch of a Community Policing program at Nine Mile Police Station, a

item.title: Music Royalties and Payments for PNG Artists
item.description: [Pawa TV] Musicians in Papua New Guinea earn through royalties, but lack a domestic collective management organisation. Many rely on overseas bodies like APRA for payments, facing challenges in enforcement and broadcaster compliance. The post Music Royalties and Payments for PNG Artists first appeared on PAWA

item.title: PNG Needs More Hospitals as PMGH Struggles With Rising Patient Numbers
item.description: [PNG Facts] Calls have been made for major health infrastructure expansion in the nation's capital after concerns emerged that Port Moresby General Hospital is being overwhelmed by patients who could be treated elsewhere. Speaking in Parliament, Abau MP Sir Puka Temu said the growing population of Port Moresby

item.title: Tegia urges disability inclusion at NCD Dialogue Forum
item.description: [Post Courier] National Capital District Diff-Abilities Advocacy Agency (NCDDAA) Operations Manager Benson Tegia has told the NCD Disability Sector Dialogue at Holiday Inn in Port Moresby that disability inclusion must be treated as a government priority. Tegia said the presence of agencies, service providers, don

item.title: ENB TB treatment success rate remains low: Doctor
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By WASITA ROYAL The East New Britain Provincial Health Authority has recorded a tuberculosis (TB) treatment success rate of just 58 percent for all forms of the disease in 2025, well below national and global targets, a five-day annual review has revealed. The review, which concluded last Friday, al

item.title: Jacksons Airport redevelopment project launched; Port Moresby is the gateway to Asia and Pacific
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The National Government and the National Airports Corporation (NAC) last Friday launched a K2.5 billion redevelopment and expansion program for Port Moresby International Airport, marking the start of one of the largest aviation infrastructure projects in Papua New Guinea’s history. Acting Managing

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Illegal logging will sink government’s downstream processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government’s ambitious plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing and value-adding will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: USP Council meeting reaffirms commitment to Pacific leadership and institutional renewal
item.description: [Education News PNG] The 101st meeting of the Council of The University of the South Pacific (USP) was convened in Nukuʻalofa in the Kingdom of Tonga on 21–22 The post USP Council meeting reaffirms commitment to Pacific leadership and institutional renewal first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby
item.description: [Inside PNG] Brothers Payne and Klese Haas clash in the M1 derby as Brisbane hosts Gold Coast. "He gives me a paragraph of stuff to work on," Klese says. The post Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Brotherly love meets rivalry in M1 derby appeared first on Inside PNG

item.title: PNG Government Targets Online Abuse Through Sevis Digital ID System
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] The Papua New Guinea Government will use its newly launched Sevis PNG Digital ID platform to help authorities identify individuals operating fake Facebook accounts and engaging in online abuse, Acting Information and Communication Technology Minister Peter Tsiamalili Jr has announced. Speaking in Pa

item.title: Electric Cars in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Electric vehicles are emerging in Papua New Guinea, with local businesses and government engaging in sustainable transport discussions. Challenges like limited infrastructure and higher costs remain, but adoption is feasible with supportive policies. The post Electric Cars in PNG first appeared on P

item.title: Student among Three Killed Following Enga Tribal Compensation Dispute
item.description: [PNG Facts] A young schoolboy and two men have lost their lives after a compensation ceremony descended into violence in Enga Province, raising fears of further tribal unrest in the area. The deaths occurred in Lagaip District where clans had gathered to settle differences through a BEL KOL payment ceremony, bu

item.title: Hidden Valley Mine CDA with State Solicitor
item.description: [Post Courier] The Community Development Agreement (CDA) for the Hidden Valley Mine in Morobe Province is currently with the State Solicitor at this stage, says Mining Minister Solen Loifa. The Mining Minister stated this when Member for Wau-Waria Marsh Narewec demanded an answer on when the CDA for Hidden Valley

item.title: Tkatchenko Applauds NCDC and Lands Department for Reclaiming Two Mile Hill Park Reserve
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] MORESBY South MP and Minister for Foreign Affairs, Justin Tkatchenko, has welcomed the successful reclamation of the Two Mile Hill Park Reserve land, announcing that the site has now been officially titled to the National Capital District Commission (NCDC) for public and recreational use. In a media

item.title: National Digital ID System to help track fake Facebook accounts, cyber bullying, Parliament told
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Government will use its newly launched Sevis PNG digital identity platform to help identify and track users behind fake Facebook accounts and online abuse, Acting Information and Communication Technology Minister Peter Tsiamalili Jr told Parliament yesterday. Responding to concerns raised by Wew

item.title: Aropa na Kieta seif long bom nau
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pipel long Central Bougainville i no ken wari tumas long ol bom bilong Wol Woa 2 nau bihain long ABG i wokbung wantaim New Zealand Defence Force long rausim ol.

item.title: ACT NOW! files second legal challenge against the PNG Forest Authority
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Second legal action against the PNG Forest Authority filed by ACT NOW

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.title: JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Government of Japan, through the Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA), has joined the Government of Papua New Guinea and the National Fisheries Authority to The post JICA and PNG Break Ground on Major Fisheries College Upgrade in Kavieng first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: A Collective Call for Papua New Guinea’s Future
item.description: [Inside PNG] Prime Minister James Marape urges citizens to demand people-focused policies over political distractions to build a stronger Papua New Guinea. The post A Collective Call for Papua New Guinea’s Future first appeared on Inside PNG . The post A Collective Call for Papua New Guinea’s Future appeared fir

item.title: PNG's Mt Hagen Airport Upgrade Paves Way for Boeing 737 and Airbus A220
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Air travellers in Papua New Guinea's Highlands Region can expect improved aviation services in the coming months as work gets underway to upgrade the runway at Mt Hagen's Kagamuga Airport. The National Airports Corporation has launched a large-scale rehabilitation program that will enable the airpor

item.title: Corporal and Capital Punishment in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea has abolished capital punishment, replacing it with life imprisonment, while corporal punishment remains legally permitted in some contexts, highlighting ongoing tensions between tradition, law reform and human rights advocacy. The post Corporal and Capital Punishment in Papua New G

item.title: PNG Government Invests K877 Million in Student Education Support
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's education sector has received a major boost, with more than 2.5 million students benefiting from the Government's tuition fee subsidy initiative since the Marape-Rosso Government took office. Prime Minister James Marape The scale of the support was outlined by Prime Minister James

item.title: Kerowagi MP demands answers on the plight of innocent PNGDF recruits left stranded by a fraud probe
item.description: [Post Courier] Member for Kerowagi, Francis Kikin Siune, has challenged Prime Minister James Marape in Parliament over the mass dismissal of military applicants from training depots, warning that hundreds of genuine, merit-based recruits have become collateral damage in the ongoing Papua New Guinea Defence Force (

item.title: NOCFS working with police to ensure suspects involved in Porgera child abuse case face the law
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE National Office for Child and Family Services (NOCFS) is working with police to ensure the suspect involved in abusing a girl in Porgera, Enga Province, is arrested and charged for child abuse. NOCFS Chief Executive Officer Mr Jerry Wap said this today after a video went viral on social media pl

item.title: Gov’t confirms new Payroll System to stop ghost workers and fraud in PNG
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Minister for Public Service, Joe Sungi said the new Dayforce Version 24 system will eliminate manual processes and create audit trails to stop ghost workers, duplicate employee records, and payroll fraud. He was responding to the questions asked by Northern Governor, Gary Juffa over the government’s

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: ACT NOW! files second legal challenge against PNG Forest Authority
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! has filed a second legal action against the PNG Forest Authority

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : A Nation In His Making – A Memory
item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae
item.description: [Education News PNG] Thirty-one cadets from the PNG Joint Forces Academy successfully completed a major field training exercise in Lae this week as part of their final military The post PNG Defence Cadets Complete Intensive Military Training Exercise in Lae first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Bird Calls on Govt to Handout Resistant Crops as El Nino Warning Peaks
item.description: [Inside PNG] Allan Bird urges PNG to distribute NARI's drought-resistant crops now as severe El Niño warnings peak, threatening over 60% of the population. The post Bird Calls on Govt to Handout Resistant Crops as El Nino Warning Peaks first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Bird Calls on Govt to Handout Resista

item.title: PNG Eyes Economic Growth Through Major LNG and Mining Projects
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea is preparing for what the Government describes as a long period of economic expansion as major resource projects move closer to development. Prime Minister James Marape says the Papua LNG project in Gulf Province and the Wafi-Golpu project in Morobe Province are among several large

item.title: The Police Tattoo
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Police Tattoo in Papua New Guinea celebrates policing tradition and cultural pride through band performances, strengthening regional cooperation and community trust, while marking a significant milestone in PNG's history. The post The Police Tattoo first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Digital ID to Help Track Fake Facebook Users
item.description: [PNG Facts] Efforts to curb fake Facebook accounts and anonymous online attacks in Papua New Guinea are set to receive a boost through the Government’s new Sevis PNG Digital ID platform. Acting ICT Minister Peter Tsiamalili Jr says the system will help law enforcement agencies and regulators verify the identiti

item.title: 2000 plus graduates have not taken up any positions
item.description: [Post Courier] Member for Karimui-Nomane Francis Alua questions the Higher Education Minister on why over 2000 students all over the country who have graduated from teacher training institutions, including the University of Goroka in 2025, have still not taken up teaching positions in many of the schools in the co

item.title: Governor Parkop calls for action: Our ocean defines our future
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] ON the eve of World Ocean Day, NCD Governor Powes Parkop yesterday led a symbolic walk from Ela Beach to Sir Hubert Murray Stadium, rallying citizens to protect Papua New Guinea’s vast ocean from plastic pollution and climate threats. The initiative drew strong community participation, sending a pow

item.title: Bougainville must demonstrate it is capable of running its own affairs
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The tabling of the Parliamentary Bougainville Committee consultation report in the National Parliament this week has reignited one of Papua New Guinea’s most important national conversations-Bougainville’s political future. As Members of Parliament debated the report, mixed reactions emerged. Some s

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: More complaints of illegal FCA logging
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities in remote Nakanai region of West New Britain say their forests are being illegally destroyed by a Malaysian logging company

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has launched its 2026 Entrepreneurial Fair, bringing together students, academics, and industry partners under the theme “AI, Innovation and The post USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus first appeared on

item.title: Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and Timor-Leste Strengthen Ties
item.description: [Inside PNG] Nobel Laureate Dr. José Ramos-Horta visits Port Moresby, backing PNG’s eco-leadership and inviting PNG musicians to Timor-Leste. A new era for Melanesian ties. The post Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and Timor-Leste Strengthen Ties first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Eco-Health and Culture, PNG and

item.title: PNG Public Service Sees Shift as Women Rise in DPLGA Leadership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] A long-standing gender gap in one of Papua New Guinea’s central government departments has been broken, following the appointment of women into senior leadership positions within the Department of Provincial and Local Level Government Affairs. PNG Public Service Sees Shift as Women Rise in DPLGA Lea

item.title: The Sacred Baining Fire Dance of East New Britain
item.description: [Pawa TV] The Baining people's Fire Dance in Papua New Guinea is a sacred night ritual, symbolising bravery and spiritual connection, marking significant life events and honouring ancestral spirits, while maintaining cultural identity. The post The Sacred Baining Fire Dance of East New Britain first appeared

item.title: Detainees Join NCDC Drive to Clean Up PNG Capital Port Moresby
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea capital Port Moresby streets received a major clean-up boost this week after the National Capital District Commission joined forces with Bomana Correctional Services to carry out a city-wide sanitation campaign aimed at improving the capital’s environment and supporting detainee reh

item.title: Health Minister welcomes Media scrutiny, vows to fix hospital overcrowding
item.description: [Post Courier] Health Minister Elias Kapavore has welcomed intense international media scrutiny of the country’s struggling healthcare system, framing the current crisis as a vital wake-up call for government intervention. ​Responding to tough questioning from Opposition Leader and Chuave MP James Nomane in Parlia

item.title: Enga PPC calls for peace following killings in Laiagam
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] ENGA Provincial Police Commander (PPC), Chief Superintendent, Steven Harris has appealed to Makol and Makol Tap communities to remain clam and let the law take its course following the killing of three people at Laiagam over the weekend. Police alleged that on the evening of Saturday 06th June 2026,

item.title: Paita calls for stronger Christian values and oversight of churches
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Morobe Governor Rainbo Paita, MP, has called on the Government to strengthen Papua New Guinea’s Christian identity and consider introducing proper oversight for religious ministries operating in the country. Mr. Paita raised the issue in Parliament sitting yesterday during Question Time when directi

item.title: Dei bai-ileksen suruk bek tupela de
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] PNGEC i wari long hevi bilong sekyuriti na i surukim bai-ileksen bilong Dei Open i go bek tupela de

item.title: Laundering cases in PNG enforce historic round log export ban
item.description: [Act Now PNG] An international financial crackdown is underway in PNG with global intelligence agency INTERPOL

item.description: [Bougainville News] President Toroama confirms ABG position on Panguna Partnering process The President of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG), Hon. Ishmael Toroama, has confirmed that the ABG has rejected the proposed partnership between Bougainville Copper Limited (BCL) and CMOC Group Limited in … Continue r

item.description: [Education News PNG] As The University of the South Pacific (USP) graduation season continues across the region, the Emalus Campus ceremony in Vanuatu today served as a powerful The post USP Pro-Chancellor challenges new generation of Pacific leadersat Emalus graduation first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Ryan Ani: From Raukele to the Barramandis
item.description: [Inside PNG] A fresh face is joining the PNG Barramandis as they head to Japan for the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup East Asia-Pacific (EAP) Qualifier. At just 22 years old, Ryan Ani is ready to make his international debut. Ani, who hails from Hula and Babaka in the Rigo Coastal area of Central Province, grew up [...

item.title: PNG Property Event Gets Major Support from BSP
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Preparations are underway for the 2026 Hausples Real Estate Show following fresh backing from BSP Financial Group, which has committed K45,000 towards the national property event. The sponsorship sees BSP return as Diamond Sponsor for the exhibition that will bring together banks, property developer

item.title: The Court System Hierarchy in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's court system, rooted in colonial history, incorporates village, district, National, and Supreme Courts, blending customary law with modern legal principles to ensure fair justice nationwide. The post The Court System Hierarchy in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG's Kagamuga Airport Upgrade to Open Door for Modern Jet Services
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's Mt Hagen Kagamuga Airport is set for a major transformation following the commencement of a runway rehabilitation project aimed at preparing the airport for larger and more modern aircraft by the end of 2026. The National Airports Corporation (NAC) announced that construction work

item.title: Opposition confronts Health Minister over K3.2 billion budget amid hospital crisis
item.description: [Post Courier] Opposition Leader and Member for Chuave, James Nomane, questioned the government in Parliament today, demanding answers on behalf of mothers forced to endure labour and maternity services on hospital floors. ​Nomane directed his questions to Health Minister Elias Kapavore, emphasising that despite t

item.title: Papua New Guinean Fulbright Scholar Brings Public Health Expertise Home After U.S. Graduate Studies
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE U.S. Embassy in Port Moresby is pleased to announce that Beverly Hunney has successfully completed her Fulbright Foreign Student Program and returned to Papua New Guinea after earning a Master’s of Public Health from the University of South Florida. Ms. Hunney’s achievement represents the strong

item.title: NCDC, Bomana Detainees join forces in Major Port Moresby clean-up
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The National Capital District Commission (NCDC), in partnership with Bomana Correctional Services, has carried out a major community clean-up exercise in Port Moresby as part of ongoing efforts to improve urban cleanliness and support detainee rehabilitation. The initiative saw 30 detainees from Bom

item.title: PMGH laik ol famili rausim bodi long mog hariap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Sif Ekseketiv Opisa bilong Pot Mosbi Jeneral Haus Sik, Dokta Paki Molumi i singaut long ol pipel long go sekim mog na rausim dai bodi bilong ol famili na wantok bilong ol hariap, na karim i go planim. Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: Bougainville News Alert 2026 : Simon Pentanu : My meeting with Francis Ona
item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

item.title: Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG icon Alex Johnston signs with PNG Chiefs for 2028. The NRL try-scoring king joins as the first local hero, honoring his proud Kumuls heritage. The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG Chiefs first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kumuls Legend Alex Johnston Seals Deal with PNG C

item.title: PNG PM Defends NEC Decision on Kroton Shares
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has pushed back against claims that the government mishandled the PNG LNG Kroton equity arrangement, telling Parliament the current structure was aimed at finally returning benefits to landowners and provincial governments. The Prime Minister was responding to accusations

item.title: The Power of the Kina
item.description: [Pawa TV] Over the past decade, Papua New Guinea's kina has faced significant depreciation due to reforms and global conditions, impacting purchasing power and increasing import costs, despite attempts to boost economic stability. The post The Power of the Kina first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Education Reform Sparks Uncertainty Over National Exams in Pilot Provinces
item.description: [PNG Facts] Students, parents and teachers in Papua New Guinea’s pilot education reform provinces are still waiting for clear guidance on whether national examinations will proceed this year, with concerns now being raised on the floor of Parliament. PNG Education Reform Sparks Uncertainty Over National Exams i

item.title: Morobe schools not ready for 1-6-6 restructure
item.description: [Post Courier] School heads across Morobe province have said that almost all their schools are not prepared for the 1-6-6 education restructure. In a special meeting between head teachers, principals, their deputies, Morobe provincial education and National Department of Education representatives, several issues f

item.title: NDC advises coastal areas resident to stay alert
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] The National Disaster Centre (NDC) and Port Moresby Geophysical Observatory advised the public, particularly residents living along the coastal areas of northern coastline of mainland PNG and New Guinea Islands, to remain alert and vigilant following reports of a magnitude 7.8 earthquake in the Phil

item.title: Marimari Lutheran Church prepares to bid farewell to historic church after 52 years
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Tears, memories, thanksgiving and anticipation filled the air at Marimari Lutheran Church on Sunday as worshippers gathered for their final regular service inside one of Port Moresby’s most historic Lutheran churches. The church, established in 1974 and serving generations of Papua New Guineans for

item.title: Australia pasim tingting bilong PNG wantaim ragbi lig
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Australia i bilip ol i pasim tingting na bel bilong Papaua Niugini pinis taim ol i sapotim PNG long putim wanpela tim insait long NRL. Ol i bilip dispela i pasim PNG na i no wari long ol arapela wokbung PNG i gat wantaim ol arapela kantri olsem China.

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.description: [Education News PNG] More than 60 outstanding students and graduates of The University of the South Pacific (USP) were recognised for academic excellence at the Medals and Prizes The post USP honours more than 60 high achievers at Medals and Prizes Ceremony first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank backs the 2026 Innovation PNG Awards, sponsoring the Small Business category to drive growth and recognize local creative entrepreneurs. The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank Backs Small Business Innovation for 2026 ap

item.title: PNG's Central Police Mobility Boosted With New Vehicle for Biura Post
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] A new police vehicle has been handed over to the Biura Police Post in Central Province as part of ongoing support from the Hiri-Koiari District Development Authority. The presentation ceremony, attended by community leaders and police officers, marked the fourth vehicle donated by the district autho

item.title: PNG’s Fastest Men
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's sprinters, notably Pais Wisil and Tovetuna Tuna, have set national records, inspiring future athletes. The country's athletics scene is thriving, raising questions about increased support for talent development. The post PNG’s Fastest Men first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Chiefs Secure England International Matty Lees for 2028 NRL Campaign
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea's push towards its historic entry into the National Rugby League has gathered further momentum with the signing of English international forward Matty Lees, adding more experience and leadership to the PNG Chiefs' foundation squad. The acquisition sees Lees become Player 004 for the

item.title: Parliament suspended amid low turnout ahead of crucial Bougainville Debate
item.description: [Post Courier] The third week of Parliament started late today when Deputy Speaker Koni Iguan was forced to suspend proceedings due to a lack of quorum, leaving the chambers nearly empty ahead of a highly anticipated debate on the Autonomous Region of Bougainville. ​Deputy Speaker Iguan suspended the session "to t

item.title: EDDA aims to build 50 single classrooms by end of 2026
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Esa’ala District Development Authority (EDDA) under the leadership of Chairman and Member for Esa’ala District, Jimmy Maladina aims to build 50 single ciassrooms within the district by the end of the year. All 50 newly bult metal structures will be able to fit up to 2.050 students from within th

item.title: PM Marape defends Constitutional Rights ahead of 2027 poll
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has reaffirmed the government’s commitment to protecting the constitutional rights of candidates and voters ahead of the 2027 National General Election. PM Marape assured Parliament that preparations are underway to deliver a free, fair, safe and credible poll. Respon

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges
item.description: [Education News PNG] Teachers and nursing colleges in Papua New Guinea (PNG) have the opportunity to apply for Institutional Development Grants (IDG) up to K150,000.00 from Australia Awards. The post Australia Awards offers development grants for colleges first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade
item.description: [Inside PNG] Kina Bank reopens its upgraded Rangeview Digital Hub on Level 1, bringing instant cards, loans, and digital support to Port Moresby shoppers. The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with Level 1 Upgrade first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Kina Bank’s Rangeview Digital Hub Returns with

item.title: PNG Labour Scheme Opens Doors for Overseas Employment After PALM Expo
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea is seeing growing interest from Australian employers seeking workers through the Pacific Australia Labour Mobility scheme following a recent recruitment and training tour in two provinces. PNG Labour Scheme Opens Doors for Overseas Employment After PALM Expo/Photo by Australian High

item.title: Community launches five-year disaster and development plan
item.description: [Post Courier] Residents of four villages in the Umbaku Ward of Morobe’s Wantoat have developed a five-year Community-Based Disaster Risk Management Plan. The initiative to launch this plan was prompted by a landslide in December 2024, which claimed four lives and caused damage to properties and income sources. To

item.title: Prime Minister Marape Invites Political Leaders To Dialogue On 2027 Election Preparation
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape has invited leaders of all political parties across government and opposition to participate in an important dialogue and awareness session on preparations for the 2027 National General Election. The meeting, scheduled for today at Melanesian Haus in Port Moresby, will br

item.title: Minister Pala questioned over delayed K5 million for new District Headquarters
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Member for Middle Fly, Mai Maso Hewabi, has questioned Minister for National Planning and Monitoring, Sir Ano Pala, over delays in the release of K5 million allocated to each of seven newly created districts for the development of district headquarters. Raising the matter during Question Time in Par

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial land leases.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: Term 2 Delay in Manus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The Manus Provincial Education Board (PEB) has issued a delay notice for the start of term 2 due to severe weather. In a memorandum to The post Term 2 Delay in Manus first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG’s National Court quashes the Ombudsman Commission’s "ultra vires" order, clearing the legal path for NICTA to resume Starlink’s satellite license review. The post National Court Overturns Ombudsman Order Blocking Starlink Licence in PNG first appeared on Inside PNG . The post National Court Over

item.title: Former Australian Officer Takes Senior PNG Police Role
item.description: [PNG Facts] EAST New Britain police will now be led by former Australian Federal Police officer Amanda Kates following her appointment as provincial police commander in Papua New Guinea. Former Australian Officer Takes Senior PNG Police Role/Photo supplied Police Commissioner David Manning announced the appoint

item.title: NCD Disability sector dialogue forum begins
item.description: [Post Courier] The National Capital District Disabilities Advocacy Agency (NCD DAA) is hosting a dialogue forum today at Holiday Inn, bringing together stakeholders and organisations to discuss the status, challenges, opportunities, and future directions of disability‑inclusive development in NCD and Papua New Gui

item.title: Buk bilong Pikinini celebrates World Environment Day with solar energy learning for children
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] BUK bilong Pikinini on Friday (05/06/26) celebrated World Environment Day with children across its Library Learning Centres through a series of fun, educational, and hands-on activities focused on environmental awareness and renewable energy under the theme “Solar Energy: Clean Light for a Healthy E

item.title: Man charged over alleged social media threats against Ok Tedi Operations
item.description: [The PNG Sun] A 34-year-old man has been arrested and charged for allegedly posting threats on social media calling for the closure of the Kiunga-Tabubil Highway and the disruption of operations linked to OK Tedi Mining Limited. Acting North Fly Provincial Police Commander Inspector Anjap Minata said the suspect

item.title: Government fails to answer UN concerns on illegal FCA logging and land abuses
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The Papua New Guinea government has failed to answer questions from the United Nations over the misuse of Forest Clearing Authority (FCA) logging licences and controversial SABL land leases.

item.title: PNG Home Buyers Encouraged as BSP Supports Hausples Expo
item.description: [PNG Facts] Thousands of Papua New Guineans planning to buy their first home are expected to benefit from this year’s Hausples Real Estate Show after BSP Financial Group renewed its partnership with the event. The bank has come in as Diamond Sponsor with K45,000 in support for the 2026 exhibition, which will be

item.title: ACT NOW! Spotlights Illegal Logging at Asia Pacific Global Dialogue, Malaysia
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! recently attended and presented on illegal logging and forest crime in Papua New Guinea at the Asia- Pacific Regional Private Sector Dialogue meeting held in Malaysia.

item.title: PNG LNG Equity Debate Sparks Heated Clash in Parliament
item.description: [PNG Facts] A fierce political showdown erupted in Papua New Guinea Parliament on Friday after the Marape-Rosso Government came under attack over its handling of the 4.27 percent Kroton equity option linked to the PNG LNG project. Prime Minister James Marape faced sharp criticism from Opposition figures, includ
